title: TARGETARCH in FROM uses the native architecture, not target
body:
### Issue Description

Related to #14375.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

I'm on an M1 Mac, so my native architecture is Arm64.

```
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM scratch AS files-amd64
FROM files-${TARGETARCH}

$ podman build --platform linux/amd64 .
```

### Describe the results you received

It interpolates `${TARGETARCH}` as `arm64` rather than `amd64`.

```
[1/2] STEP 1/1: FROM scratch AS files-amd64
--> 0e8c37aa7061
[2/2] STEP 1/1: FROM files-arm64
Trying to pull docker.io/library/files-arm64:latest...
```

### Describe the results you expected

`$TARGETARCH` should actually be the target architecture, not the native one. 

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

comments.body:
This seems like the relevant change to buildah: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4061

There's a test case that should not be passing for cross-platform builds: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/tests/bud/base-with-arg/Containerfile

However, if you use:

```
ARG TARGETARCH
FROM platform-${TARGETARCH} AS final
...
```

Then it works as expected.
I'll check thanks @rgov for the investigation.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This issue does not persists anymore on upstream, please feel free to re-open this.
